exemplar.title:
To what extent does the addition of dried Citrus reticulata (mandarin) 
peel (0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 grams) affect the growth of Raphanus sativus
(radish) plants as measured by shoot length (cm) after 3 weeks?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does the addition of dried Citrus reticulata (mandarin) 
peel (0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 grams) affect the growth of Raphanus sativus
(radish) plants as measured by shoot length (cm) after 3 weeks?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated in a specific, authentic context of fruitâwaste utilization and mandarin peel application
Clear explanation of key methodological considerations (variables, replicates, controls, ethics, statistical needs)
Procedure described in sufficient detail to allow reproduction with few ambiguities
weaknesses: Omission of local soil characteristics and ambient environmental conditions
Lack of specification for peelâdrying protocol (time, temperature)
Control of light intensity not quantified (e.g., lux or PAR values)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (research question context and methodological considerations) clearly meet the 5-6 descriptors, but the methodology description falls just short, with a few ambiguities preventing full reproducibility. An overall score of 4 reflects strong, coherent design with some missing detail.

strengths: Data tables and graphical outputs are clearly presented with consistent units and significant figures
Statistical processing (averages, SD, IQR, ANOVA, Tukey HSD) is carried out accurately and reported rigorously
Communication of statistical choices (hypotheses, test selection) is succinct and appropriate
weaknesses: Measurement uncertainties are stated but not propagated into calculations or displayed on graphs
Error bars or confidence intervals are missing from figures to show variability
Some processing details (e.g., derivation of table boundaries) are omitted, and narrative overstates significance despite non-significant pairwise results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data processing is accurate (5-6 band) and communication is clear (3-4 band), but limited consideration of uncertainties (1-2 band) reduces the overall profile. A holistic score of 4 balances these strengths and omissions.

strengths: Conclusion is relevant to the research question and reflects overall trends in the data
Comparison with external studies (Chae et al., El-Serafy) situates findings within the scientific context
weaknesses: Conclusion does not fully integrate the non-significant Tukey results into its justification
Discussion of broader mechanisms or literature contradictions is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both conclusion strands align with the 3-4 band but lack full consistency and depth. A score of 3 reflects a relevant, partially supported conclusion with some contextual linkage.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and linked to data validity (duration, sample size, missing pH analysis, single growth parameter)
Improvements proposed are realistic and directly tied to the limitations, with explanations of how they would enhance validity
weaknesses: The relative impact of each limitation is not fully explained
No explicitly labeled improvements section, leading to slightly reduced clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation strands both fall within the 5-6 band for specificity and relevance, but minor omissions (explicit sectioning and detailed impact explanations) warrant a score of 4 rather than the maximum.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states a conclusion (moderate peel improves growth, high peel inhibits) that is **largely consistent with mean trends but not fully with the non-significant Tukey results**. Thus, the conclusion is relevant yet only partially supported, fitting the 3-4 band at 3.

Data tables, graph and statistical outputs are clearly laid out with units; headings and significant figures are generally consistent, though the outlier table is partly blank and graph axes lack error bars. Communication is therefore **clear but not fully precise**, meriting the 3-4 band at 4.

Averages, standard deviations, IQR, one-way ANOVA and Tukey HSD are correctly executed and tabulated; calculations are internally consistent with raw data. Minor interpretation slips (claiming âclearly beneficialâ despite non-significant pairwise results) do not affect processing accuracy, so the work sits in the 5-6 band, score 5.

Specific weaknesses (short duration, small sample size, absence of soil pH analysis, single growth parameter) are listed **with explanations of how each could have affected statistical significance or nutrient availability**, demonstrating clear articulation of relative impact. This meets the 5-6 band; modest omissions warrant a 5.

Two external studies are summarised and linked to the experimentâs findings, showing **some relevant comparison** to accepted scientific context. The discussion lacks depth on underlying mechanisms or contradictory literature, so the strand aligns with mid 3-4 band (score 3).

The research question is not only clearly stated but is embedded within a focused discussion of fruit-waste disposal at the studentâs boarding school, the nutritional composition of mandarin peel, and the suitability of radish as a model species. This places the investigation in a **specific, authentic and scientifically appropriate context**, matching the 5-6 descriptor. Minor contextual gaps (e.g. no reference to local soil conditions) prevent awarding the top of the band.

Uncertainties for ruler (Â±0.1 cm) and balance (Â±0.1 g) are stated, but they are **not propagated, displayed on the graph, nor incorporated into statistical tests**. Evidence of consideration is therefore limited, matching the top of the 1-2 band.

Improvements (longer study, more replicates, soil analysis, pH control, measuring biomass) are directly tied to identified limitations and **explained in terms of how they would enhance data validity or interpretation**, satisfying the 5-6 descriptor at score 5.

The student **explains key methodological considerations**: independent/dependent variables, six treatment levels, five replicates, control of water, light and pot size, risk/ethics, and the need for ANOVA to handle multiple means. Explanations link each factor to data sufficiency or validity (e.g. fast-growing radish allows observable change; five plants reduce random error). A few areas (ambient temperature, peel drying consistency) are not treated, so the evidence fits solidly but not perfectly at 5 in the 5-6 band.

The procedure lists masses, soil mass, watering regime, planting depth and replicate numbers, enabling near-full reproducibility. **Some ambiguities remain** (exact light intensity/location, peel dryness standard, daily temperature range), so the description is reproducible "with few omissions", fitting the 3-4 band; the stronger end (4) is warranted because most essential details are present.

annotations.comment: The focus on Vitamin C concentration is justified, but the text does not address its degradation rate in soil over three weeks, potentially affecting validity.
The research question is clearly stated within the context of fruit-waste utilization at the boarding school and mandarin peel application, demonstrating specific and authentic context.
The context omits details on local soil characteristics and environmental conditions which could influence applicability and reproducibility of results.
Introduction effectively establishes motivation but would benefit from referencing baseline soil nutrient levels or pH to strengthen context relevance.
The methodology description lacks specification of the peel drying protocol (time, temperature), which is crucial to ensure consistency and reproducibility.
Independent and dependent variables tables are detailed with units and uncertainties, aiding clarity, but lack mention of initial soil moisture.
Risk assessment table lists hazards and controls well but omits controls for standardizing peel moisture content, which could introduce variability.
Ethical considerations are thorough, detailing plant welfare and scientific integrity to ensure reliable data collection without manipulation.
The note on reusing pots and returning soil to the eco garden demonstrates commendable sustainability practice.
Control variables are appropriately identified, but the method for equalizing light intensity lacks quantification (e.g., lux or PAR values).
Equipment list is comprehensive and directly supports reproducibility of the procedure by listing all necessary materials.
Procedure is outlined step-by-step, but lacks specifics on light source location and intensity, limiting full reproducibility.
The raw data table is clearly presented with consistent units, but does not indicate measurement uncertainties alongside each data point.
The post-experiment image effectively shows plant growth but lacks error bars or confidence intervals to depict variability.
Statistical analysis description succinctly defines Hâ and Hâ and explains choice of one-way ANOVA, demonstrating clear communication of processing.
Table 6 header appears but no actual calculation details are shown, leaving unclear how boundaries were derived.
Figure 1 presents average shoot length trends clearly, but the narrative claim that results are âclearly beneficialâ overlooks the non-significant pairwise findings.
ANOVA results are accurately tabulated with SS, df, MS, F, P-value, and F crit, reflecting rigorous and precise processing.
Tukey HSD table is comprehensive, but the wide confidence intervals should be discussed to contextualize lack of pairwise significance.
Discussion conclusion summary is relevant to the research question but does not fully integrate the non-significant Tukey results into justification.
Comparison with Chae et al. and El-Serafy studies effectively situates findings in scientific context, showing relevant linkage.
Mechanistic explanation of nutrient release and pH effects is thoughtful, but would be stronger with measured soil pH data to support claims.
Limitations are specific and well articulated, but the relative impact of each (e.g., short duration vs sample size) is not explained.
No explicit improvements section is provided; the student should propose realistic, targeted modifications (e.g., include soil pH analysis) linked to identified limitations.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Data processing quality

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
